title: Plutarch, Moralia: Whether the Affections of the Soul are Worse Than Those of the Body
description: Essay from Plutarch's Moralia: Whether the Affections of the Soul are Worse Than Those of the Body.
introduction: Essay from Plutarch's Moralia: Whether the Affections of the Soul are Worse Than Those of the Body.

translation.text: From Plutarch's 'Whether the Affections of the Soul are Worse Than Those of the Body': In this essay, Plutarch examines this important aspect of the moral life with his characteristic combination of erudition, anecdote, and practical wisdom.
